title: CHAINLINK_INSTALL_SHOWN global AtomicBool — no way to reset, leaks 'once' state across tests and sessions
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

**Dimension:** State Management / Testability

**Location:** /home/doll/OpenClaudia/src/tools/chainlink.rs line 7 (CHAINLINK_INSTALL_SHOWN), lines 43-51 (swap-and-branch)

**Violation:**
A process-global AtomicBool controls whether the 'chainlink not found, install from github' message has been shown. Once the swap fires, future chainlink-missing errors return the shorter 'Chainlink not available.' message (line 51). Consequences:
1. **Tests interact.** Running test A before test B changes the message test B sees. Bandaid: #[serial_test].
2. **Long-running sessions never re-show the helpful message** even after a plausible event (system update, PATH change) that might have reinstalled chainlink.
3. **There is no supply-chain hygiene on the install URL** — the message hard-codes https://github.com/dollspace-gay/chainlink and directs the model (and by extension the user) to trust that URL. If the repo is ever transferred, the message still points to the old URL.

**Mandated Refactor:**
1. Move the 'shown' flag onto SessionState (removing the global).
2. Re-check PATH for chainlink every 5 minutes or at session start; show the install message whenever chainlink is actually absent, not 'first time per process'.
3. Make the install URL configurable via AppConfig — agents deploying in restricted environments can point at an internal mirror.
